name: Rivery Data Pipeline API
what_it_does: Cloud-native data operations platform with REST API — enabling data teams to build ELT pipelines, run SQL transformations, orchestrate workflows, and perform reverse ETL from data warehouses to business applications, with a logic-based river model that combines ingestion, transformation, and orchestration in a single unified platform.
use_cases: Building ELT pipelines via Rivery API for source-to-warehouse data ingestion automation
Running SQL transformations via Rivery API for warehouse-native data transformation
Orchestrating pipeline workflows via Rivery API for complex dependency-based data flows
Managing reverse ETL via Rivery API for warehouse-to-business-tool data activation
Triggering pipeline runs via Rivery API for event-driven data workflow execution
not_for: Real-time streaming (Rivery is primarily batch/scheduled ELT)
Teams needing open-source self-hosted (Rivery is SaaS-only)
Complex custom connector development beyond available connectors
best_when: A data team wants a unified ELT + transformation + orchestration + reverse ETL platform in one SaaS tool, avoiding the complexity of separate tools for each layer.
avoid_when: You need real-time streaming, open-source self-hosted, or only need simple source-to-destination ETL without orchestration.

auth.notes: API key (Personal Access Token) via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Rivery account settings. REST API at api.rivery.io/v1/. Account ID (environment ID) required for some endpoints. Webhooks for river run events. Rivers (pipelines) identified by river IDs. Multiple environments (dev/staging/prod) with separate API keys.

pricing.notes: Israeli company (Tel Aviv). Founded 2018 by Itamar Ben Hemo and Yoav Bornstein. $32M+ funding (MizMaa Ventures, Glilot Capital). Rivers: core concept — ingestion river, transformation river, storage river, target river, logic river. Logic rivers: orchestration layer combining other rivers. 200+ connectors. Targets: Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks. Multi-environment: dev/staging/prod with environment cloning. Version control: river versioning for change management. Monitoring: run history, alerts, SLA monitoring. Used by: mid-market data teams. SaaS-only: no self-hosted option.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. River (pipeline) model: ingestion/transformation/target/logic rivers. Account ID for some endpoints. Webhooks for events. Israeli company. SOC2/GDPR. 200+ connectors. Multi-environment support. SaaS-only.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: River reads idempotent by river ID. River runs: trigger by river ID — not natively idempotent (check run status before triggering). Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: River model: core concept — Rivery calls pipelines 'rivers' with types (source, target, transformation, logic)
Environment separation: dev/staging/prod have separate API keys and river IDs — manage carefully
Logic rivers: orchestration layer — trigger logic rivers to run dependent rivers in sequence
River IDs: obtain river IDs via the Rivery UI or LIST API before triggering runs
SaaS-only: no self-hosted option — all data flows through Rivery's infrastructure
Israeli company: data residency in US or EU — specify region during account setup for GDPR

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token API key. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. Israeli company: EU data residency option for GDPR. Source/destination credentials stored in Rivery — significant trust surface. SaaS-only: data flows through Rivery's network. Environment separation reduces blast radius per API key. Webhooks: verify webhook authenticity. No OAuth2 for fine-grained access.
